audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim inscribed-stone, Ebal-altar, blessing/curse assembly, and twelve-curse text, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for plastered Torah stones, uncut-stone altar, peace-offering rejoicing, Israel's renewed peoplehood, Gerizim/Ebal tribal arrangement, and communal assent to curses against hidden abuse and injustice.

verses.restored: Moses and elders of Israel commanded people, saying, Keep all command I command you today.
It shall be, on day you cross Jordan into land YHWH your Divine gives you, you shall raise for yourself great stones and plaster them with plaster.

You shall write upon them all words of this Torah/instruction when you cross, so you may enter land YHWH your Divine gives you, land flowing with milk and honey, as YHWH Divine of your fathers spoke to you.
It shall be, when you cross Jordan, you shall raise these stones, concerning which I command you today, on Mount Ebal, and plaster them with plaster.
There you shall build altar to YHWH your Divine, altar of stones; you shall not wield iron upon them.
Whole/uncut stones you shall build altar of YHWH your Divine, and offer upon it burnt offerings to YHWH your Divine.
You shall sacrifice peace/well-being offerings and eat there, and rejoice before YHWH your Divine.
You shall write upon stones all words of this Torah very plainly/well explained.
Moses and Levitical priests spoke to all Israel, saying, Be silent and hear, Israel: this day you have become a people to YHWH your Divine.
You shall listen to voice of YHWH your Divine and do his commands and statutes that I command you today.
Moses commanded people on that day, saying,
These shall stand to bless people upon Mount Gerizim when you cross Jordan: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph, and Benjamin.
These shall stand for curse upon Mount Ebal: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.
Levites shall answer and say to every man of Israel with loud voice:
Cursed is man who makes carved or molten image, an abomination to YHWH, work of hands of craftsman, and sets it in secret. All people shall answer and say, Amen.
Cursed is one treating his father or mother with contempt. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one moving boundary marker of his neighbor. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one causing a blind person to wander in way. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one bending judgment of sojourner, orphan, and widow. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one lying with wife of his father, for he uncovered skirt/covering of his father. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one lying with any beast. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one lying with his sister, daughter of his father or daughter of his mother. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one lying with his mother-in-law. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one striking his neighbor in secret. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one taking bribe to strike a soul/life of innocent blood. All people shall say, Amen.
Cursed is one who does not uphold words of this Torah/instruction to do them. All people shall say, Amen.

translation_notes: Torah is written publicly and plainly on monumental stones at the threshold of the land.
The altar uses uncut stones untouched by iron, while peace offerings join covenant inscription to communal eating and joy.
Gerizim and Ebal distribute tribes across blessing and curse geography; the chapter records only the spoken curse formulas.
Each curse receives communal Amen, making the people witnesses against hidden wrongdoing rather than passive hearers.

The list targets secret idolatry, contempt within families, land theft, deliberate endangerment of a blind person, judicial abuse of vulnerable residents, sexual violations, hidden assault, and bribed killing.
The command protecting a blind traveler must not become an ableist metaphor that treats blindness as moral ignorance.
The final curse concerns failure to uphold Torah in practice, not magical malediction against imperfect people.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 27 for magical cursing, ableist language, family abuse disguised as parental honor, or persecution over private belief. Center public accessibility of law, protection of land and disabled travelers, justice for immigrants and bereaved households, and communal refusal of secret violence and bribed killing.
